name: The Sierra de Bernia — Circular from Olla Altea through the Sierra de Oltá
name_original: La Sierra de Bernia — Circular desde Olla Altea por la Sierra de Oltá
region_intro: Mountain biking around Altea offers diverse and challenging terrain, blending mountainous backdrops with coastal views. The region is characterized by prominent mountain ranges like Sierra de Bernia and the dramatic cliffs of Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada. Riders can expect a mix of challenging climbs, technical single tracks, and thrilling descents across dry, dusty trails. This area provides a varied landscape for mountain biking, from high peaks to coastal paths. Best mountain bike trails around Altea The most popular mountain bike trail is The Sierra de Bernia — Circular from Olla Altea through the Sierra de Oltá, a 28.5 miles (45.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 24 minutes to complete. This route features challenging climbs and panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Serra Gelada and Benidorm MTB Loop, a difficult 30.2 miles (48.6 km) path. This route is known for its coastal views and significant elevation changes within the protected natural park. Local mountain bikers also love the Margoig Trail – La Nucia – Singletrack at El Margoig loop from Altea, a 27.6 miles (44.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain with singletrack sections, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Mountain biking around Altea is defined by mountainous backdrops, coastal paths, and natural park landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain treks to routes with technical single tracks. The routes in Altea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 1500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Altea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Altea?
A: Altea offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 39 routes available. These range from easier paths to very challenging rides, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Altea?
A: The mild climate of Altea makes it ideal for mountain biking year-round, but spring and autumn are particularly pleasant. The weather is generally conducive to outdoor activities, offering comfortable temperatures for exploring the diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Altea?
A: You can expect a diverse range of terrain, from challenging climbs in prominent mountain ranges like Sierra de Bernia to dramatic coastal paths in Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada. Trails often feature dry, dusty surfaces, technical single tracks, and thrilling descents, all set against a backdrop of mountainous vistas and stunning sea views.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Altea?
A: While Altea is known for its challenging routes, there are also easier trails available. Out of the 39 routes, 3 are classified as easy, providing options for those new to mountain biking or looking for a more relaxed ride. The Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada, for instance, offers some less demanding paths.

Q: Where can I find challenging mountain bike routes near Altea?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Altea has 19 difficult mountain bike routes. Notable options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e926111915/the-sierra-de-bernia-circular-from-olla-altea-through-the-sierra-de-olta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Sierra de Bernia — Circular from Olla Altea through the Sierra de Oltá&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation gain and panoramic views, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e925884022/serra-gelada-and-benidorm-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra Gelada and Benidorm MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt;, known for its coastal views and demanding sections.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Altea area?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Altea are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32034628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Margoig Trail – La Nucia – Singletrack at El Margoig loop from Altea&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain and singletrack sections.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails around Altea?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, it's always best to check specific park regulations, especially within protected areas like Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada or Sierra de Bernia. Keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are well-behaved around other trail users and wildlife.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, Altea offers routes suitable for families. While some trails are very challenging, there are easier paths, particularly within the Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada, such as those leading to the Albir lighthouse, which can be enjoyed by families looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking near Altea?
A: Mountain biking around Altea offers access to stunning natural attractions. You can encounter the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/149038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Forat de Bèrnia&lt;/a&gt;, a natural tunnel through the Sierra de Bernia, or enjoy views of the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/174441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón de Ifach summit&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albir Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also offers beautiful coastal vistas.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks accessible by mountain bike in Altea?
A: Yes, the region around Altea is rich in history. Cyclists can encounter historical sites such as the 16th-century Fort of Bernia, which offers a glimpse into the area's past. The charming old town of Altea itself, with its cobbled streets and whitewashed houses, is also a cultural attraction worth exploring.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Altea?
A: The mountain bike trails in Altea are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse and challenging terrain, the stunning Mediterranean sea views, and the unique blend of mountainous backdrops with coastal scenery.

Q: Is there public transport access to mountain biking trailheads in Altea?
A: Altea has local bus services that connect different parts of the town and surrounding areas. While direct access to all trailheads by public transport might be limited, you can often reach central points or villages close to trail starts. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for specific routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Altea?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Altea, particularly in the town center and near popular starting points for trails. For routes heading into natural parks or mountainous areas, look for designated parking spots near trailheads or in nearby villages. Always ensure you park responsibly and legally.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in Altea's natural parks?
A: Generally, mountain biking on established trails within natural parks like Parque Natural de la Serra Gelada or Sierra de Bernia does not require specific permits for individual riders. However, it's always good practice to respect park rules, stay on marked trails, and be aware of any specific regulations that might apply to larger groups or organized events. For more information on the region, you can visit &lt;a href="https://visitaltea.es/altea/web_php/index.php?contenido=showPois&id=47&idNivel=430&lang=48" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;visitaltea.es&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Beautiful route on gravel, with no further difficulty. The effort of the journey is rewarded with a great view. The Mirador Tossal del Navarro is a popular destination for outdoor enthusiasts as it offers stunning panoramic views and a connection to the rich history and geography of the region. The tour is worthwhile for anyone looking for peace, nature and adventure.

Site with amazing views towards the Serra de Bèrnia and its impressive walls. It is reached by paved road and there is parking for cars. From here we start the ascent along the path towards Fort de Bèrnia. A few meters away is a restaurant with beautiful views.

Very picturesque and entertaining path that allows you to reach the Fort de Bernia. It is a trail designed for hiking but it can be ridden on a mtb although it has some technical sections with a lot of loose stones and the slope to overcome is high so it can be done without rushing... the views are totally amazing once you get there to the ruins of the fort.
The most picturesque and entertaining section is the one that runs through the Morro del Penyal next to the cliff with impressive views.
Totally recommended for both mtb and hiking!

waypoints.name: View of the Sierra de Bernia and the coast
